expectations.text: Report opens with a prose narrative summary (5-10 lines) that a professor can read in 60 seconds
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'Contexto Fisiopatologico' (expository, not synthesis). 'Sumario Executivo' (Section 11) is at the END and is tables, not prose. No upfront narrative synthesis.

expectations.text: Report contains at least 1 specific actionable recommendation for what to change or keep in teaching material
expectations.evidence: Section 11 has clinical hierarchy (CSPH → carvedilol, varizes → NSBB/LEV) but never addresses teaching material, slides, lectures, or what to change in educational content.

expectations.text: Report contains findings from at least 2 distinct search legs
expectations.evidence: Uses PubMed MCP (3 searches), SCite (2 searches), Consensus (1 search), WebSearch (5 queries) — 4 distinct source types, though not structured as named legs.

expectations.text: Report includes a comparative table with an 'implication' column explaining what each finding means
expectations.evidence: Section 11 'Dados Concretos' table has Medida/Valor/IC/Tempo/Fonte but NO implication column. No cross-source comparison table.

expectations.text: Report interprets convergences in prose (not just '3/3 = ALTA') explaining WHY convergence matters
expectations.evidence: No convergence/divergence analysis. Sources are integrated into a single narrative. Never compares what different sources found independently.

expectations.text: Report contains at least 3 PMIDs or DOIs with verification status
expectations.evidence: 13 PMIDs: 10 VERIFIED via PubMed MCP, 2 WEB-VERIFIED, 1 corrected (15867408→16306522). Excellent verification work including error correction.

expectations.text: Report includes concrete effect sizes with confidence intervals
expectations.evidence: Throughout: PREDESCI HR 0.51 (0.26-0.97), NNT=9; carvedilol MD 1.08 mmHg (0.61-1.54); CAVARLY HR 0.37 (0.192-0.716); combo RR 0.39 (0.19-0.76).

expectations.text: Report does NOT contain vague claims without specific citation
expectations.evidence: Most claims cited with PMID. Section 1 '10-15% ao ano' cited D'Amico PMID 16298014. A few contextual statements lack PMID but are supporting text, not clinical claims.
